title: What Neon Genesis Evangelion literally means
display_title: What Neon Genesis Evangelion literally means
wikitext:
[]: What "Neon Genesis Evangelion" literally means.             Quote Post by Stryker » Mon Mar 21, 2011 3:28 pm So, I decided to look up "Neon", "Genesis", and "Evangelion", and then mish mash it all together to make what would be Neon Genesis Evangelion. Neon : Neon ( /ˈniːɒn/) is the chemical element that has the symbol Ne and an atomic number of 10. Genesis : A coming into being. Evangelical (Since Evangelion leads directly to NGE, and that isn't what I am looking for): The term evangelical has its etymological roots in the Greek word for "gospel" or "good news": ευαγγελιον (evangelion), from eu- "good" and angelion "message." (Since Evangelical is the idea of good news, it must mean that Evangelion must be someone/something that spreads good news.. right?) So, as I see it, "Neon Genesis Evangelion" literally translates into this: A messenger spreads good news about the new chemical element that comes to being. But, of course, there are several meanings, therefore it can be interpreted differently. Show your literal/wacky translations of "Neon Genesis Evangelion" means. (Please, tell us something we don't know. Something away from the lines of "Neon Genesis Evangelion (新世紀エヴァンゲリオン Shin Seiki Evangerion?) franchise is an umbrella of Japanese media properties generally owned by the anime studio Gainax.")

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Mar 21, 2011 3:31 pm http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=3430

[]:             Quote Post by toe mash » Mon Mar 21, 2011 4:16 pm inb4 the obligatory pic from the extra curricular lesson.. You know the one I'm talking about.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Mar 21, 2011 9:46 pm Mr. Tines wrote: http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=3430

[]:             Quote Post by Allemann » Tue Mar 22, 2011 2:55 pm "The New Gospel of Creation" Where's the old one, Anno?

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Tue Mar 22, 2011 3:00 pm "Shinseiki Evangelion" New Century Evangelion. There. Afaik, the english name was only chosen because it sounded cool.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Mar 22, 2011 3:19 pm Quick! Somebody post NaveryW's hilarious mp3 edit of Greenfield explaining the meaning!

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Tue Mar 22, 2011 3:26 pm toe mash wrote: inb4 the obligatory pic from the extra curricular lesson..

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Tue Mar 22, 2011 3:27 pm Guys, I know that Shinseiki Evangelion means New Century Evangelion, and that "Neon" in the name isn't referring to the chemical element, but one thing I don't know is who the hell NaveryW is. I know exactly what Neon Genesis Evangelion means. I just looked up every single word in the name "Neon Genesis Evangelion" and made a plot based off those definitions. Now, what I am asking you to do is the same. There are different meanings to each word, so I want to see how crafty/creative/witty you can get with the plots.

[]:             Quote Post by liquidus118 » Tue Mar 22, 2011 5:02 pm InstrumentalityOne wrote:

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Thu Mar 24, 2011 10:12 pm Stryker wrote: Guys, I know that Shinseiki Evangelion means New Century Evangelion, and that "Neon" in the name isn't referring to the chemical element, but one thing I don't know is who the hell NaveryW is. I know exactly what Neon Genesis Evangelion means. I just looked up every single word in the name "Neon Genesis Evangelion" and made a plot based off those definitions. Now, what I am asking you to do is the same. There are different meanings to each word, so I want to see how crafty/creative/witty you can get with the plots.

[]:             Quote Post by Rei Makes You Go Splat » Fri Mar 25, 2011 8:50 pm Aw man. I always thought the title meant that the Evangelions were to bring about the Neon Genesis (new beginning) for humanity through the Third Impact, but now I know it was just to sound cool. Sometimes I like my own interpretation better than the creators, or the translators in this case.

[]:             Quote Post by Shogo-Kun » Fri Mar 25, 2011 9:49 pm Gospel of the New Beginning ( aka Mankind is Surely Fucked: The Series)

[]: Re: What "Neon Genesis Evangelion" literally means.             Quote Post by SaiyanPrince1994 » Fri Dec 08, 2017 9:07 am I've always prefered "First book of the new gospel."

[]: Re: What "Neon Genesis Evangelion" literally means.             Quote Post by DarkBluePhoenix » Fri Dec 08, 2017 12:15 pm SaiyanPrince1994 wrote: I've always prefered "First book of the new gospel."

[]: Re: What "Neon Genesis Evangelion" literally means.             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Fri Dec 08, 2017 11:28 pm It is easy to skip over the opening verse of Mark’s Gospel: “The begin­ning of the good news [evangelion] of Jesus Christ, the Son of God.” But if Mark is (as is widely thought) the first Gospel writer, this is an extra­ordinary sentence. For it is Mark who chooses the word “evan­gelion” to describe the story of Jesus. This choice underlines the sub­versive nature of the message. In the world of Mark’s first readers, the word that we translate as “gospel” was used for imperial proclamations. Until Mark bor­rowed the term and applied it to the story of Jesus, an evangelion meant the announcement of the birth of a new emperor, important events in the emperor’s life, or news of his victories in military battles (Theo­logical Dictionary of the New Testa­­ment, Eerdmans, 2006). In contrast, Mark applies the term evangelion to the story of a Jewish subject, living under Roman occupa­tion, and crucified by that empire as a common criminal.

[]: Re: What "Neon Genesis Evangelion" literally means.             Quote Post by Asuka'sBigBrother » Sat Dec 09, 2017 8:53 am So.... A chemically made being that is preached to be good by religious demagogues... In other words, instrumentality? Clever name.

[]: Re: What "Neon Genesis Evangelion" literally means.             Quote Post by Lavinius » Sat Dec 09, 2017 10:39 pm I wrote: by the way, said Greek name has to be Νέων Γένεσις Εὐαγγελίων, with Neon and Evangelion in the genitive plural. you'd assume that it'd be Νέον Γένεσις Εὐαγγέλιον, with them being nominative singular, but that makes absolutely no sense. the word order is a little strange in the other one, with the noun and adjective separated, but Greek poetry can do that, at least, and it has a meaning viz. "Origin of New Evangelia", which differs from the translation I've usually seen attributed viz. "Birth of a New Gospel" only in number, which naturally is occluded in Japanese

[]: Re: What "Neon Genesis Evangelion" literally means.             Quote Post by robersora » Mon Dec 11, 2017 8:19 pm We all know Anno chose the title because it sounded pretentious. I would love to know how much he knew about the the term "Evangelion" before using it.

[]: Re: What "Neon Genesis Evangelion" literally means.             Quote Post by DarkBluePhoenix » Mon Dec 11, 2017 9:34 pm robersora wrote: We all know Anno chose the title because it sounded pretentious. I would love to know how much he knew about the the term "Evangelion" before using it.

. In the world of Mark’s first readers, the word that we translate as “gospel” was used for imperial proclamations.
